hypothesis: Incorporating a fast learned surrogate model predicting DFT-derived formation energy and electronic-conductivity proxies into the reinforcement-learning reward for REINVENT will yield generated substrates that, when validated by full DFT, show at least a 20% higher fraction of low-formation-energy, conductive cyclic frameworks than RL optimized only on the expert heuristic filters, while simultaneously reducing the incidence of policy collapse and maintaining sample uniqueness above 50%.
